title: Trace the execution path end-to-end for a single user request across the model, prompt, retrieval, and orchestration layers.
prompt:
Trace the execution path end-to-end for a single user request across the model, prompt, retrieval, and orchestration layers.

*Answer in the editor as you would out loud in an interview (a few sentences to a short paragraph is fine). Then use 💡 Hint, 🧠 AI feedback, or 📗 Show answer.*
reference_solution:
Example: an enterprise assistant query, "What's our policy on parental leave for part-time employees?" (1) Orchestration input — receives the request, checks auth/rate limits, loads chat history, and routes it to RAG. (2) Retrieval processing — expands synonyms, embeds the query, vector-searches top-k chunks, and filters by ACLs. (3) Prompt assembly — combines the system prompt, the query, history, and the retrieved document chunks with citations. (4) Model inference — the model synthesizes the policy chunks into a coherent, accurate answer citing source docs. (5) Post-processing — validates format and citations, logs the response, updates history, and delivers the answer to the user. (6) Async monitoring — asynchronously runs quality evaluators, computes token costs, and checks anomaly detectors.

**Takeaway:** A single user turn touches all four layers in sequence, plus an async monitoring step that runs after the response is already delivered — any of those six stages is a place a failure can be introduced.
hint_notes: 6-stage trace: orchestration input → retrieval processing → prompt assembly → model inference → post-processing → async monitoring.
